pageContent:
ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Complaint — Noise Dispute
URGENCY: High
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: Owner in Lot 14 reporting persistent late-night noise from upstairs neighbour, ongoing for 3 weeks
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: Hi, I've been getting noise complaints from the unit above me every night after 10pm. This has been going on for 3 weeks and nothing has been done. My lot is 14 at 22 Harbour Street.
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Issue formal noise by-law breach notice to Lot 15 (the unit directly above Lot 14 at 22 Harbour St). Log the complaint in the dispute register. Follow up with both parties within 48 hours. If repeated, escalate to NCAT mediation referral.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: Dear Maria, thank you for raising this concern. We take noise complaints very seriously and have logged your report against Lot 15. We will issue a formal notice to the owner today and follow up with you within 48 hours to confirm the outcome. Please continue to document any further incidents with dates and times.

metadata.resolution: by-law notice issued, resolved within 7 days

pageContent:
ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Maintenance Request — Common Area
URGENCY: Critical
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: Water leaking from ceiling in common corridor, Level 3, possible pipe burst
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: There is water dripping from the ceiling on Level 3 near the lift. It started an hour ago and is getting worse. Can someone come urgently?
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Dispatch emergency plumber immediately (use preferred contractor list — 24hr contact: Rapid Plumbing 0412 555 890). Notify building manager on-site. Isolate water supply to affected riser if safe to do so. Document with photos before repairs begin. Notify all affected lot owners within 2 hours.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: Thank you for alerting us immediately. We are treating this as an emergency and have dispatched our contracted plumber who will be on-site within the hour. Please avoid the area near the leak and ensure no electrical appliances are near the water. We will keep you updated.

metadata.resolution: plumber on-site in 45 mins, pipe joint replaced

pageContent:
ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Financial Enquiry — Overdue Levy
URGENCY: Medium
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: Lot owner asking why their quarterly levy increased by 20% without prior notice
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: I just received my levy notice and it's gone up by $180 compared to last quarter. Nobody told me about this. Can you explain?
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Pull the levy schedule and AGM minutes for the current financial year. Confirm whether a special levy or budget increase was passed. Send the owner a copy of the relevant AGM resolution and updated levy schedule. If the increase was not properly approved, escalate to the strata committee immediately.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: Thank you for getting in touch. I can see your levy notice has changed from the previous quarter. This increase was approved at the Annual General Meeting held on [DATE] — I will email you a copy of the relevant resolution and the updated levy schedule within one business day. Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have further questions.

ENQUIRY CATEGORY: New Client Enquiry — Switching Managers
URGENCY: Low
CLIENT STATUS: new
SUMMARY: Strata committee chairperson exploring switching from current manager to new provider, 48-lot building
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: Hi, we are a 48-lot residential building in Surry Hills and we're looking to change strata managers. Our current contract is up for renewal in 3 months. What does your service include and how do we make the switch?
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Assign to Business Development. Send the new client information pack and fee proposal template. Schedule a discovery call within 2 business days. Request the current management agreement and financial statements from the prospect so we can prepare a competitive proposal.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: Thank you for reaching out! We'd love to learn more about your building and how we can support your committee. Our service covers financial management, by-law enforcement, maintenance coordination, AGM facilitation, and 24/7 owner support. I'll have one of our senior managers reach out to you within 24 hours to arrange a no-obligation consultation — would that work for you?

ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Legal or Compliance Enquiry — By-Law Breach
URGENCY: High
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: Owner reports neighbour has installed an unapproved pet and is refusing to comply with no-pets by-law
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: My neighbour in Lot 8 has a large dog which is not allowed under our by-laws. I have raised it with them directly and they told me to mind my own business. What can you do?
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Review the current by-laws to confirm pet restrictions. Issue a formal breach notice to Lot 8 with a 14-day compliance period. If unresolved, refer to NCAT (NSW Civil and Administrative Tribunal) for an order. Document all correspondence. Notify the strata committee of the formal action taken.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: Thank you for bringing this to our attention. We have reviewed the by-laws for your building and can confirm that the keeping of pets in Lot 8 appears to be in breach. We will issue a formal notice to the owner of Lot 8 today, allowing 14 days to comply. If the breach continues, we will escalate to tribunal proceedings. We will keep you updated at each stage.

metadata.resolution: breach notice issued, owner removed pet within 14 days

pageContent:
ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Support Request — Portal Access
URGENCY: Low
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: New owner cannot log in to the owner portal after purchasing a lot, credentials not received
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: I recently purchased Lot 22 at 55 Park Avenue. I was told I'd get login details for the online portal but I haven't received anything. Can you help?
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Verify the ownership transfer in the system against the lot conveyancing documents. Create a new portal account for the owner. Send a welcome email with login credentials, a guide to the portal features, and a copy of the current levy schedule and by-laws. Complete within 1 business day.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: Congratulations on your new property! Welcome to the building. It looks like your portal account hasn't been set up yet — this sometimes happens when the conveyancing settlement details haven't come through to us. I'll create your account today and send your login credentials along with a welcome pack to this email address. You should receive it within a few hours.

metadata.resolution: account created and welcome email sent same day

pageContent:
ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Complaint — Management Dissatisfaction
URGENCY: High
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: Committee member formally complaining that maintenance requests are not being actioned and emails go unanswered for weeks
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: We have raised three maintenance issues in the past month and not one has been actioned. Our emails take weeks to get a response if we get one at all. This is unacceptable and we are considering changing managers.
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Escalate immediately to the Portfolio Manager or Director. Pull the open maintenance request log and identify the 3 overdue items. Assign each to a contractor with a completion deadline. Call the committee member personally within 2 hours. Provide a written service recovery plan within 24 hours with timelines for each outstanding item.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: I sincerely apologise for the experience you've had. This is not the standard of service we hold ourselves to, and I completely understand your frustration. I have personally escalated your three open maintenance requests and will be calling you this afternoon to walk through the action plan for each one. You will receive a written update by end of business tomorrow.

metadata.resolution: director call made, all 3 items resolved within 5 days, client retained

pageContent:
ENQUIRY CATEGORY: Maintenance Request — Lift Fault
URGENCY: Critical
CLIENT STATUS: existing
SUMMARY: Building lift has been out of service for 2 days, elderly and disabled residents unable to access upper floors
ORIGINAL MESSAGE: The lift has been broken for 2 days now. We have elderly residents on the upper floors who cannot use the stairs. This is a serious safety issue.
RECOMMENDED ACTION: Treat as a safety emergency. Contact lift maintenance contractor immediately for emergency repair (not a scheduled visit). Check if any residents are currently stranded and arrange temporary welfare checks. Notify all owners and tenants of the outage and estimated repair time. Document incident for insurance. If repair > 24h, arrange temporary mobility assistance for vulnerable residents.
SUGGESTED RESPONSE: We are treating this as an urgent safety matter. Our lift maintenance contractor has been contacted for emergency attendance today. In the meantime, please let us know if any residents require immediate welfare assistance so we can coordinate support. We will send a building-wide update within the hour and keep you informed of the repair timeline.

metadata.resolution: emergency repair completed within 6 hours, welfare checks done
